key_points: An industry is an economic activity concerned with the production of goods, extraction of resources, or provision of services
Industries are classified into primary, secondary, and tertiary sectors
Primary industries extract natural resources such as mining and fishing
Secondary industries manufacture finished goods from raw materials
Tertiary industries provide services such as transport, banking, and trade
examples: Primary industry example: Mining and fishing
Tertiary industry example: Banking and transport services
Agro-based industry example: Cotton textile industry
Mineral-based industry example: Iron and steel industry
important_terms: Industry: Economic activity that produces goods or services
Raw Materials: Basic materials used in industrial production
Primary Industry: Industry that extracts natural resources
Secondary Industry: Industry that converts raw materials into finished products
Tertiary Industry: Industry that provides services
exam_tips: Remember the three main sectors of industries: Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary
Understand the difference between raw materials and finished goods
Learn examples of agro-based and mineral-based industries
Remember the components of an industrial system: inputs, processes, and outputs
Understand how industries contribute to economic development
